documentation:
*********
Craft CLI
*********

A Command Line Client builder that follows the `Canonical's Guidelines for a Command Line
Interface <https://discourse.ubuntu.com/c/design/cli-guidelines/62>`_.

The library provides two main functionalities:

- a framework to define and execute application commands, which involves argument parsing and the provision of help texts

- infrastructure to handle the terminal and present all the outputs for the different application needs


Documentation
-------------

The `documentation <https://craft-cli.readthedocs.io>`_ is available on Read The Docs.


Contributing
------------

See the `<HACKING.rst>`_ document for details on how to contribute.


License
-------

Free software: GNU Lesser General Public License v3

classes.name: craft_cli.errors.CraftError

class_variables.name: craft_cli.errors.CraftError.message
documentation:
The main message to the user, to be shown as first line (and probably only that,
according to the different modes); note that in some cases the log location will be
attached to this message.

class_variables.name: craft_cli.errors.CraftError.details
documentation:
The full error details received from a third party which originated the error
situation.

class_variables.name: craft_cli.errors.CraftError.resolution
documentation:
An extra line indicating to the user how the error may be fixed or avoided (to be
shown together with `message`).

class_variables.name: craft_cli.errors.CraftError.docs_url
documentation: An URL to point the user to documentation (to be shown together with `message`).

class_variables.name: craft_cli.errors.CraftError.doc_slug
documentation:
The slug to the user documentation. Needs a base url to form a full address.
Note that `docs_url` has preference if it is set.

class_variables.name: craft_cli.errors.CraftError.logpath_report
documentation:
Whether the location of the log filepath should be presented in the screen as the
final message.

class_variables.name: craft_cli.errors.CraftError.reportable
documentation: If an error report should be sent to some error-handling backend (like Sentry).

class_variables.name: craft_cli.errors.CraftError.retcode
documentation: The code to return when the application finishes.
Signal a program error with a lot of information to report.

classes.name: craft_cli.errors.CraftCommandError
documentation:
A CraftError with precise error output from a command.

This exception class augments CraftError with the addition of a `stderr`
parameter. This parameter is meant to hold the standard error contents of
the failed command - as such, it sits between the typically brief "message"
and the "details" parameters from the point of view of verbosity.

It's meant to be used in cases where the executed command's standard error
is helpful enough to the user to be worth the extra text output.

classes.name: craft_cli.errors.ArgumentParsingError
documentation: Exception used when an argument parsing error is found.

classes.name: craft_cli.errors.ProvideHelpException
documentation: Exception used to provide help to the user.

functions.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin.init_emitter
documentation:
Ensure `emit` is always clean, and initiated (in test mode).

Note that the `init` is done in the current instance that all modules already
acquired.

This is an "autouse" fixture, so it just works, no need to declare it in your tests.

documentation: Provide a helper to test everything that was shown using the Emitter.

classes.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin._RegexComparingText
documentation: A string that compares for equality using regex.match.

classes.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin.RecordingEmitter

methods.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin.RecordingEmitter.pause
documentation: Mimics the pause context manager, storing the state to simplify tests.

methods.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin.RecordingEmitter.record
documentation: Record the method call and its specific parameters.

methods.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin.RecordingEmitter._check
documentation: Really verify messages.

methods.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin.RecordingEmitter.assert_message
documentation:
Check the 'message' method was properly used.

It verifies that the method was called at least once with the expected text.

If 'regex' is True, the expected text will be used as a regular expression.

methods.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin.RecordingEmitter.assert_progress
documentation:
Check the 'progress' method was properly used.

It verifies that the method was called at least once with the expected text (with
the given 'permanent' flag).

If 'regex' is True, the expected text will be used as a regular expression.

methods.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin.RecordingEmitter.assert_verbose
documentation:
Check the 'verbose' method was properly used.

It verifies that the method was called at least once with the expected text.

If 'regex' is True, the expected text will be used as a regular expression.

methods.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin.RecordingEmitter.assert_debug
documentation:
Check the 'debug' method was properly used.

It verifies that the method was called at least once with the expected text.

If 'regex' is True, the expected text will be used as a regular expression.

methods.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin.RecordingEmitter.assert_trace
documentation:
Check the 'trace' method was properly used.

It verifies that the method was called at least once with the expected text.

If 'regex' is True, the expected text will be used as a regular expression.

methods.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin.RecordingEmitter.assert_messages
documentation:
Check that the 'message' method was called several times with the given texts.

This is helper for a common case that happen in multiline commands results
where 'message' is called several times.

methods.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin.RecordingEmitter.assert_interactions
documentation:
Check that the expected call list happen at some point between all stored calls.

If None is passed, asserts that no message was emitted.

Record what is shown using the emitter and provide a nice API for tests.

This class is NOT meant to be used directly, please use the `emitter` fixture instead
which provides an instance of this class with context properly set up.

methods.name: craft_cli.pytest_plugin._RecordingProgresser.advance
documentation: Record the advance usage.

classes.name: craft_cli.printer._Spinner

methods.name: craft_cli.printer._Spinner.supervise
documentation: Supervise a message to spin it if it remains too long.

methods.name: craft_cli.printer._Spinner.stop
documentation: Stop self.

A supervisor thread that will repeat long-standing messages with a spinner besides it.

This will be a long-lived single thread that will supervise each message received
through the `supervise` method, and when it stays too long, the printer's `spin`
will be called with that message and a text to "draw" a spinner, including the elapsed
time.

The timing related part of the code uses two constants: _SPINNER_THRESHOLD is how
many seconds before activating the spinner for the message, and _SPINNER_DELAY is
the time between `spin` calls.

When a new message arrives (or None, to indicate that there is nothing to supervise) and
the previous message was "being spinned", a last `spin` call will be done to clean
the spinner.

methods.name: craft_cli.printer.Printer.spin

methods.name: craft_cli.printer.Printer.show

methods.name: craft_cli.printer.Printer.progress_bar
documentation: Show a progress bar to the given stream.

methods.name: craft_cli.printer.Printer.stop
documentation:
Stop the printing infrastructure.

In detail:
- stop the spinner
- show the cursor
- add a new line to the screen (if needed)
- close the log file

methods.name: craft_cli.printer.Printer.set_secrets
documentation: Set the list of strings that should be masked out in all outputs.

Handle writing the different messages to the different outputs (out, err and log).

If TESTMODE is True, this class changes its behaviour: the spinner is never started,
so there is no thread polluting messages when running tests if they take too long to run.

A list of these is what is passed to the `Dispatcher` to run commands as part

documentation:
Identifier of the argument (the reference in the dictionary returned) by the
`Dispatcher.pre_parse_args()` method)

The argument type: `flag` for arguments that are set to `True` if specified
(`False` by default), or `option` if a value is needed after it.

class_variables.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.GlobalArgument.short_option
documentation:
The short form of the argument (a dash with a letter, e.g. `-s`); it can be None
if the option does not have a short form.

class_variables.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.GlobalArgument.long_option
documentation: The long form of the argument (two dashes and a name, e.g. `--secure`).

class_variables.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.GlobalArgument.help_message
documentation: the one-line text that describes the argument, for building the help texts.
Definition of a global argument to be handled by the Dispatcher.

classes.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.BaseCommand

methods.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.BaseCommand.fill_parser
documentation:
Specify command's specific parameters.

Each command parameters are independent of other commands, but note there are some
global ones (see `main.Dispatcher._build_argument_parser`).

If this method is not overridden, the command will not have any parameters.

```
param parser

The object to fill with this command's parameters.
```

methods.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.BaseCommand.run
documentation:
Execute command's actual functionality.

It must be overridden by the command implementation.

```
param parsed_args

The parsed arguments that were defined in .

return

This method should return None or the desired process' return code.
```
NOTE: run() returns `Optional[int]` instead of `int | None` as the latter would
be a breaking change for subclasses that override this with just `None` and
use the `overrides.override` decorator. See:
https://github.com/mkorpela/overrides/issues/115

class_variables.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.BaseCommand.name
documentation: The identifier in the command line, like "build" or "pack".

class_variables.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.BaseCommand.help_msg
documentation: A one-line help message for user documentation.

class_variables.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.BaseCommand.overview
documentation: Longer, multi-line text with the whole command description.

class_variables.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.BaseCommand.common
documentation:
Whether this is a common/starter command, which are prioritized in the help
(defaults to False).

class_variables.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.BaseCommand.hidden
documentation:
Do not show in help texts, useful for aliases or deprecated commands (defaults
to False).

Base class to build application commands.

Subclass this to create a new command; the subclass must define the `name`,
`help_msg`, and `overview` attributes. Additionally, it may override the
`common` and `hidden` attributes to change from their default values.

The subclass may also override some methods for the proper command behaviour (see each
method's docstring).

Finally, the subclass must be declared in the corresponding section of command groups
indicated to the Dispatcher.

classes.name: craft_cli.dispatcher._CustomArgumentParser

methods.name: craft_cli.dispatcher._CustomArgumentParser.error
documentation: Show the usage, the error message, and no more.
ArgumentParser with custom error manager.

methods.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.Dispatcher._build_no_command_error
documentation: Build the error help text for missing command, providing options.

methods.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.Dispatcher._parse_options
documentation: Parse arguments.

methods.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.Dispatcher.pre_parse_args
documentation:
Pre-parse sys args.

Several steps:

- extract the global options and detects the possible command and its args
- validate global options and apply them
- validate that command is correct (NOT loading and parsing its arguments)

If provided, `app_config` is passed to the command to be validated.

methods.name: craft_cli.dispatcher.Dispatcher.run
documentation: Really run the command.

Set up infrastructure and let the needed command run.

♪♫"Leeeeeet, the command ruuun"♪♫ <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cv-0mmVnxPA>

```
param appname

the name of the application

param commands_groups

a list of command groups available to the user

param summary

the summary of the application (for help texts)

param extra_global_args

other automatic global arguments than the ones
provided automatically

param default_command

the command to run if none was specified in the command line

param docs_base_url

The base address of the documentation, for help messages.
```

functions.name: craft_cli.messages._get_log_filepath
documentation:
Provide a unique filepath for logging.

The app name is used for both the directory where the logs are located and each log name.

Rules:
\- use an platformdirs provided directory
\- base filename is \<appname>.\<timestamp with microseconds>.log
\- it rotates until it gets to reaches {data}`._MAX_LOG_FILES`
\- after limit is achieved, remove the exceeding files
\- ignore other non-log files in the directory

Existing files are not renamed (no need, as each name is unique) nor gzipped (they may
be currently in use by another process).

functions.name: craft_cli.messages._get_traceback_lines
documentation: Get the traceback lines (if any) from an exception.

functions.name: craft_cli.messages._active_guard
documentation:
Decorate Emitter methods to be called when active.

It will check that the emitter is initiated and that is not stopped (except when
ignore_when_stopped=True, in that case the call will be ignored, to support
double-ending).

classes.name: craft_cli.messages._PipeReaderThread

methods.name: craft_cli.messages._PipeReaderThread._write
documentation: Convert the byte stream into unicode lines and send it to the printer.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages._PipeReaderThread._run_posix
documentation: Run the thread, handling pipes in the POSIX way.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages._PipeReaderThread._run_windows
documentation: Run the thread, handling pipes in the Windows way.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages._PipeReaderThread.run
documentation: Run the thread.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages._PipeReaderThread.stop
documentation:
Stop the thread.

This flag ourselves to quit, but then makes the main thread (which is the one calling
this method) to wait ourselves to finish.

Under Windows it inserts an extra byte in the pipe to unblock the reading.

A thread that reads bytes from a pipe and write lines to the Printer.

The core part of reading the pipe and stopping work differently according to the platform:

- posix: use `select` with a timeout: if has data write it to Printer, if the stop flag
    is set just quit

- windows: read in a blocking way, so the `stop` method will write a byte to unblock it
    after setting the stop flag (this extra byte is handled by the reading code)

classes.name: craft_cli.messages._StreamContextManager
documentation: A context manager that provides a pipe for subprocess to write its output.

```
when in BRIEF mode.
```

methods.name: craft_cli.messages._Handler.emit

A logging handler that emits messages through the core Printer.

classes.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.init
documentation:
Initialize the emitter; this must be called once and before emitting any messages.

```
param streaming_brief

Whether informational messages should be streamed with
progress messages when using BRIEF mode (see example 29).

param docs_base_url

The base address of the documentation, for error reporting
purposes.
```

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.get_mode
documentation: Return the mode of the emitter.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.set_mode
documentation: Set the mode of the emitter.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.message
documentation:
Show an important message to the user.

Normally used as the final message, to show the result of a command.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.verbose
documentation:
Verbose information.

Useful to provide more information to the user that shouldn't be exposed
when in brief mode for clarity and simplicity.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.debug
documentation:
Debug information.

To record everything that the user may not want to normally see but useful
for the app developers to understand why things are failing or performing
forensics on the produced logs.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.trace
documentation:
Trace information.

A way to expose system-generated information, about the general process or
particular information, which in general would be too overwhelming for
debugging purposes but sometimes needed for particular analysis.

It only produces information to the screen and into the logs if in TRACE mode.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter._get_progress_params
documentation: Calculate the different parameters for progress information.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.progress
documentation:
Progress information for a multi-step command.

This is normally used to present several separated text messages.

If a progress message is important enough that it should not be overwritten by the
next ones, use 'permanent=True'.

These messages will be truncated to the terminal's width, and overwritten by the next
line (unless verbose/trace mode).

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.progress_bar
documentation:
Progress information for a potentially long-running single step of a command.

E.g. a download or provisioning step.

Returns a context manager with a `.advance` method to call on each progress (passing the
delta progress, unless delta=False here, which implies that the calls to `.advance` should
pass the total so far).

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.open_stream
documentation: Open a stream context manager to get messages from subprocesses.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.pause
documentation:
Context manager that pauses and resumes the control of the terminal.

Note that no messages will be collected while paused, not even for logging.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter._stop
documentation: Do all the stopping.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.ended_ok
documentation: Finish the messaging system gracefully.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter._report_error
documentation: Report the different message lines from a CraftError.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.error
documentation: Handle the system's indicated error and stop machinery.

methods.name: craft_cli.messages.Emitter.set_secrets
documentation: Set the list of strings that should be masked out in all output.

Main interface to all the messages emitting functionality.

This handles everything that goes to screen and to the log file, even interfacing
with the formal logging infrastructure to get messages from it.

This class is not meant to be instantiated by the application, just use `emit` from
this module.

The user of this object will select any of the following methods according to what
to show:

- `message`: for the final output of the running command; if there is important information
  that needs to be shown to the user in the middle of the execution (and not overwritten
  by other messages) this method can be also used but passing intermediate=True.

- `progress`: for all the progress messages intended to provide information that the
  machinery is running and doing what.

- `trace`: for all the messages that may used by the *developers* to do any debugging on
  the application behaviour and/or logs forensics.

documentation: Provide all help texts.

functions.name: craft_cli.helptexts._build_item_plain
documentation:
Prepare an item for the help in plain format, generically a title and a text aligned.

This is how the plain mode is built:
- the title starts in column 4 with an extra ':', aligned to the right
- the text starts in 4 plus the title space; if too wide it's wrapped.

functions.name: craft_cli.helptexts.process_overview_for_markdown
documentation:
Process a regular overview to be rendered with markdown.

In detail:

- Join all lines for the same paragraph (as wrapping is responsibility of the renderer)

- Dedent and wrap with triple-backtick all indented blocks

Paragraphs are separated by empty lines

classes.name: craft_cli.helptexts.HelpBuilder

methods.name: craft_cli.helptexts.HelpBuilder.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the help builder.

```
param appname

The name of the application.

param general_summary

A summary of the application.

param command_groups

The CommandGroups for the application.

param docs_base_url

The base URL for the documentation.
```

methods.name: craft_cli.helptexts.HelpBuilder.get_usage_message
documentation:
Build a usage and error message.

The command is the extra string used after the application name to build the
full command that will be shown in the usage message; for example, having an
application name of "someapp":
- if command is "" it will be shown "Try 'appname -h' for help".
- if command is "version" it will be shown "Try 'appname version -h' for help"

The error message is the specific problem in the given parameters.

methods.name: craft_cli.helptexts.HelpBuilder.get_full_help
documentation:
Produce the text for the default help.

- global_options: options defined at application level (not in the commands),
  with the (options, description) structure

The help text has the following structure:

- usage
- summary
- common commands listed and described shortly
- all commands grouped, just listed
- more help and documentation

methods.name: craft_cli.helptexts.HelpBuilder.get_detailed_help
documentation:
Produce the text for the detailed help.

- global_options: options defined at application level (not in the commands),
  with the (options, description) structure

The help text has the following structure:

- usage
- summary
- global options
- all commands shown with description, grouped
- more help and documentation

methods.name: craft_cli.helptexts.HelpBuilder._build_plain_command_help
documentation:
Build the command help in its plain version.

The help text has the following structure:

- usage
- summary
- positional arguments (only if parameters are not empty)
- options
- other related commands
- help for all commands and documentation

methods.name: craft_cli.helptexts.HelpBuilder._build_markdown_command_help
documentation:
Build the command help in its markdown version.

The help text has the following structure:

- usage
- summary
- positional arguments (only if parameters are not empty)
- options
- other related commands

methods.name: craft_cli.helptexts.HelpBuilder.get_command_help
documentation:
Produce the text for each command's help in any output format.

- command: the instantiated command for which help is prepared

- arguments: all command options and parameters, with the (name, description) structure;
    note that any argument with description being `HIDDEN` will be ignored

- output_format: the selected output format

The help text structure depends of the output format.
Produce the different help texts.
